prompt_text: Open source or affordable HR tools instead of Breathe
response:
# Open Source & Affordable HR Tools

## **Free/Open Source**

| Tool | Best For | Cost |
|------|----------|------|
| **OpenHRMS** | Complete HR suite | Free (open source) |
| **Frappe ERPNext** | HR + Finance integration | Free (open source) |
| **Odoo Community** | HR + accounting | Free (self-hosted) |
| **Workday Community** | Mid-market alternative | Free tier available |

## **Affordable SaaS ($5-50/employee/month)**

| Tool | Features | Price |
|------|----------|-------|
| **Zoho People** | Payroll, attendance, leaves | $2-5/user/month |
| **BambooHR** | Onboarding, performance | $99-299/month |
| **Namely** | Core HR + engagement | ~$8-10/employee |
| **Paycor** | Payroll-focused | $99-500/month |
| **Wave** | Payroll only | Free + per-run fees |

## **Best Budget-Friendly Combos**

1. **Spreadsheet + Zoho**: Google Sheets + Zoho People for basics
2. **Self-hosted**: OpenHRMS on your server (minimal hosting cost)
3. **Startup Stack**: Google Workspace + Guidepoint + Rippling (limited free tier)

## **What to prioritize** (in order)
- Payroll & tax compliance
- Leave/attendance tracking
- Document management
- Performance tracking (can add later)

**How many employees?** This affects the best choice significantly.
